description: Adding a lookalike package name that impersonates a popular dependency, pulling malicious code into the tree.
body: Failure mode: a misspelled or scope-swapped package name is added (or auto-suggested) and installs a typosquat that mimics a trusted library while running malicious install scripts or exfiltrating data. Detection signals: a newly-published, low-download package with a near-identical name to a popular one; repo URL missing or pointing to a recent fork; unexpected install-time network activity. Do not install a package whose name closely resembles a known one without verifying it. Safer fallback: confirm the canonical name, maintainer, and repository, install from the verified source, and remove the impostor. Risk boundary: deny the add and escalate when a lookalike package shows typosquat signals rather than installing to test it.
